name: Westmark
description_short: Idaho-based credit union with 70+ years of service, $1.6B in assets, and 16 local branches offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgages to 81,000+ members.
description_long:
Westmark Credit Union has served Idaho communities for over 70 years as a member-owned, NCUA-insured financial institution. The organization has grown to manage $1.6 billion in assets and serve more than 81,000 members across 16 local branches, primarily in the Greater Idaho Falls area. This deep regional roots and community presence define their operational model.

Westmark offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, RV loans, personal loans (Cobalt Edge and Lifestyle loans), and term certificates. They also provide digital banking through mobile apps and online platforms, bill pay services, and financial wellness tools including budgeting calculators and goal-tracking features. Mortgage services include both first mortgages and refinancing options, with current rates advertised as low as 5.283% APR for first mortgages.

The credit union distinguishes itself through local branch presence, personalized member service, and active community engagement. They awarded 153 scholarships in 2025, donated $223,255 to local causes, and logged 1,619 volunteer hours. Member testimonials emphasize personalized service, staff knowledge by name, and staff willingness to go beyond standard service. The organization positions itself as "as local as it gets" with a mission focused on improving members' social and economic lives.

As a credit union rather than a bank, Westmark is member-owned and non-profit, meaning returns theoretically benefit members rather than shareholders. However, the organization's reach is geographically limited to Idaho, which restricts membership and service area. Competitiveness of rates requires comparison against larger regional and national lenders, and some services (like business banking) are mentioned but not detailed.
services: Checking accounts with no surprise fees and mobile access
Savings accounts and share certificates (term certificates up to 3.95% APY advertised)
First mortgage loans (APR as low as 5.283%)
Home equity lines of credit (APR as low as 4.99%)
Personal loans - Cobalt Edge (APR as low as 11.25%) and Lifestyle Loans (APR as low as 11.49%)
Online and mobile banking with bill pay functionality
Financial wellness suite with budgeting tools and goal tracking
Free financial literacy classes for children and adults
pros: NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 standard coverage
New checking account bonus of $150 for opening new accounts
16 local branches with staff who provide personalized service and member recognition
Comprehensive mortgage offerings with advertised rates starting at 5.283% APR for first mortgages
No surprise fees on checking accounts (explicitly stated)
Robust community support with 153 scholarships awarded in 2025 and $223K+ in annual donations
Mobile banking and digital wallet access for accounts, loans, and bill pay
cons: Geographic limitation to Idaho only, restricting membership eligibility to those living or working in the state
Specific APR and term details not fully disclosed on website; rates require application or contact to confirm
Limited business banking details provided, despite offering business accounts
No mention of alternative banking services like check cashing, money orders, or ATM network outside Westmark branches
Website does not disclose membership fees, minimum balance requirements, or savings account rates
best_for: Idaho residents seeking personalized banking with local branch relationships and community-focused values
First-time homebuyers and refinancing customers in Idaho wanting mortgage rates starting at 5.283% APR
Consumers prioritizing no-surprise-fee checking accounts with mobile banking and rewards programs
Members interested in credit unions that actively invest earnings back into local scholarships and community
diagnosis: Westmark is best for Idaho residents seeking a full-service credit union with strong local presence, personalized service, and demonstrated community commitment. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Idaho membership, making it unavailable to out-of-state consumers despite competitive rates and community-focused operations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Westmark — Credit Union in Idaho Falls, ID | CreditDoc
meta_description: Westmark — NCUA-insured credit union in Idaho Falls, ID. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
